prerequisites: A local Airbyte deployment running via Docker. The Local CSV destination is meant to run on a local workstation and does not work on Kubernetes.
Docker Desktop file sharing configured to allow access to /tmp (and /private on macOS), since output files are written under the container's local mount.
A value for the required destination_path config field, which always begins with /local (mapped to the LOCAL_ROOT environment variable, default /tmp/airbyte_local).

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The Local CSV destination writes files directly to the local filesystem and requires no API key, token, or credentials. The connector descriptor defines no secret fields, consistent with the official Airbyte documentation.

setupSteps.title: Enable Docker file sharing for the local mount
setupSteps.body: Because this connector writes to the host filesystem, open Docker Desktop Settings, go to Resources then File sharing, and add /tmp (and /private on macOS) so the container can write output. This connector runs only on a local workstation and is not supported on Kubernetes.

setupSteps.title: Set the required destination_path
setupSteps.body: Configure destination_path, the only required field. The path must begin with /local, which maps to the LOCAL_ROOT environment variable (default /tmp/airbyte_local). For example, /local/cars/models writes output to /tmp/airbyte_local/cars/models on the host. Each stream produces its own CSV file.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set the delimiter type
setupSteps.body: Configure the optional delimiter_type field to choose the character that separates values in the output CSV files. Leave it unset to use the connector default.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve the output files
setupSteps.body: Files under /tmp/airbyte_local are temporary and do not persist across Docker restarts. To keep copies, use docker cp airbyte-server:/tmp/airbyte_local <local path> to copy the generated CSV files onto your host machine.

sources.title: Browsing Output Files of Local Destinations | Airbyte Documentation
